items.title: Dragonhunter Low Intensity Power 30k/32.5k/33.5k
items.cleaned_transcript:
For the zero-button rundown, and in general with all of these, just auto attack and try not to cancel your auto attack chain. You want to make sure you get that third hit off and get that big symbol off.

For the two-button rundown, your opener is Procession of Blades, which is your trap, into your F1, which is Spear of Justice. You want to time Procession of Blades right before you cast your F1. Basically, just try to time these two skills together. If you do not time them together, the DPS loss is not significant. I think you lose somewhere between 500 and maybe 1,000 DPS. It does not really matter. If you decide to press Procession of Blades off cooldown whenever it is up, you are just losing out on a tiny bit of damage, but if you want to min-max, time Procession of Blades with your F1.

For the three-button rundown, it is the same thing as the two-button. You want to try to time Procession of Blades with your F1. Your opener is Procession of Blades into your F1, and cast Test of Faith whenever it is off cooldown. Again, you want to try to time Procession of Blades with your F1, but if you press Procession of Blades before F1 is up, the DPS loss is not significant. You will probably lose around 500 to 1,000 DPS. If you decide to turn your brain off and cast Procession of Blades whenever it is up, that is fine too. It is not a big deal.

Test of Faith is a small DPS increase for one button, and you do not change the way you play at all. You just press it whenever it is off cooldown. Triggering this gives you Aegis, and it also triggers Relic of the Dragonhunter. Basically, you get 10% more damage for 5 seconds whenever you trigger this trap. If you really want to min-max, you can keep an eye on the Dragonhunter relic icon above the enemy. For example, if I put down Test of Faith and it does not proc, then put down Procession of Blades, you will see a little sword icon above the golem. That means the Dragonhunter relic is active. If you really want to min-max, you could time Test of Faith by watching that icon and cast Test of Faith as a filler when it is no longer up. You will gain a DPS increase, but it probably will not be that significant.

For the build rundown, I will go through all the skills. On hammer, Hammer 2 is a leap. It is really nice to get around. Hammer 3 is an immob, but not really that useful. Hammer 4 is a CC, so make sure to use Hammer 4 if you see a break bar. Hammer 5 technically is also CC, but the opponent basically has to pass through it, so it is maybe situationally useful. Hammer 4 is your big CC button.

This build runs Radiance. If you look through Radiance, you will see the trait Righteous Instincts. Resolution increases your chance to critically strike. Basically, having the Resolution boon on you increases your crit chance by 25%. This is how you crit cap. If you do not have Resolution, you will experience a pretty major DPS loss.

You may be asking how you gain Resolution. You can press your heal skill off cooldown. If you are playing in a group with low Resolution uptime, your healer does not give it to you, or your comp is bad and does not give you Resolution, you can press your heal skill off cooldown. You can replace Test of Faith with, for example, Stand Your Ground! to grant yourself Resolution. Hallowed Ground is also another skill that grants yourself Resolution. Keep an eye on your Resolution uptime and make sure that you have enough Resolution uptime and are critting. If you are not, then try to substitute a little bit.

I will probably leave a link in the description, but there was a variant of this build where you would play Virtues. Virtues is a trait line that you replace Radiance with, and you basically take all of the top traits in Virtues. After testing it out, it seems like they have hammered Virtues quite a bit, and there is very little to no DPS difference between the two, so by default I go with Radiance.

The reason I do not go with Virtues by default, though you can still play Virtues, is that it is going to require a whole bunch of assassin's gear. Check the link in the description. The reason I do not recommend it is because of Unscathed Contender, where you gain a damage increase with Aegis and a damage increase while being above a certain health threshold. I assume that anyone playing this build is probably going to be a little newer, maybe learning the game, and maybe taking a lot of damage and a lot of hits. You are probably not going to have high Aegis uptime, and you may be taking hits and losing health. That is why it is not the default build.

The nice thing about Virtues, even though the damage is now very similar to Radiance with 100% Resolution uptime, is that Virtues does not rely on Resolution. If you decide to play this build and you do not like dealing with Resolution, then check out the Virtues build in the description. It is good. You will probably crit cap, and you do not have to rely on your teammates, Resolution, or looking at any of that. It is still viable, and you still play it the exact same way. The only difference if you play Virtues is that your F1 is going to come off cooldown much quicker because virtues gain reduced recharge. You will be able to use your Procession of Blades and F1 combo a little bit faster. Again, it is viable and very similar in terms of damage, but it is not ahead in damage anymore. It is just an option that you can play.

For everything else, nothing is really that important. It is just damage, damage, damage. You pretty much do not want to touch any of these at all.

For your utilities, I would probably recommend you keep Litany of Wrath. The way this works is that 33% of the damage you deal is returned to you as healing. You can effectively look at this as 6 seconds of invulnerability with how much damage you do. You can face-tank hits and heal yourself really well with Litany of Wrath. I would recommend keeping this skill and using it. If you are playing Radiance, make sure that you spam Litany of Wrath. It has a really short cooldown. Guardian has a lot of really nice utilities.

I should also mention the F skills. Whenever you use F1 with this build, you want to leave it inside the target. You have the ability to recast it, and there is going to be this Justice effect on the target ticking. Do not recast this, because otherwise you will lose your damage from Big Game Hunter. Just leave it in there. If you read it, it says that striking an enemy tethered by your Spear of Justice increases strike damage dealt by 25%, so leave it in there.

F2 is mobility, so you can get around a little quicker and faster. F3 gives Aegis, so you can block attacks from the front. This is useful if you are taking a lot of hits or damage. You can pop this and continue auto attacking with hammer instead of dodging.

For utilities, you can run Dragon's Maw. Dragon's Maw is a CC, so feel free to run Dragon's Maw instead of Signet of Courage. If you decide not to press your elite at all, then run Signet of Courage to get a little bit of extra healing, but otherwise take Dragon's Maw. You can always take Feel My Wrath! if you really want, but this is mostly for superspeed, kind of a niche use if you need to get around. The Quickness and Fury are not going to be as useful because you should have a healer, but it is nice to get around if you need that superspeed.

Bane Signet is CC. You probably do not want to swap this unless you absolutely need to. I would not swap Bane Signet. Procession of Blades I would not swap. With the two-button and three-button builds, you run Relic of the Dragonhunter, which works with your traps, so you want to have at least one trap. If you decide to swap something, swap Test of Faith.

Guardian has a bunch of really nice utility. Judge's Intervention lets you blink to your target and burn them. You select a target, and you blink to it. Merciful Intervention lets you shadowstep to an ally, effectively blinking to an ally. You can pretty much ignore the healing part, or you can use it, but it can be nice to get around if you are out of position and want to get to your allies quickly.

Wall of Reflection is useful because there are a ton of mechanics with projectiles that you can block or reflect. Sometimes you actually do not want to reflect, so watch out for that and listen to your commander if you decide to reflect and are not supposed to.

Hallowed Ground was already mentioned for Resolution uptime. It is also a pretty nice stability skill, so it is kind of two in one. You can consider running it if you want. Sanctuary is a big, good CC skill. The way it works is that you put down Sanctuary, and each pulse does a little bit of CC. Usually Sanctuary will push enemies away from wherever you put it down, but each pulse can do some CC, so it can be a nice CC skill to run. Stand Your Ground! gives extra stability. You could also run Hammer of Wisdom for CC, but you probably do not need it.

I think that is it for this build. I hope you all see success with it and enjoy.

items.title: Firebrand Low Intensity Condi 31.5k/34.5k
items.cleaned_transcript:
For the three-button breakdown, all you want to do is use Symbol of Vengeance and Zealot's Flame, and make sure to use Zealot's Fire, the second cast of your torch skill, whenever it's up. Use Mantra of Flame pretty much off cooldown. To make it really simple, time skill two with skill four so you use skill four right after skill two. Then you do not have to think about it much, because it becomes one big action.

For the five-button version, I decided to give you a tome variant. It is similar to the three-button build: use Mantra of Flame off cooldown and time skill four right after skill two. The added tome skills you care about are Tome of Justice skills two, four, and five. Right after you use Symbol of Vengeance, and skill four if it is up, swap into tome. Tome skill two, Igniting Burst, has an eight-second cooldown, similar to Symbol of Vengeance, so more or less every time you use axe two, tome two will be up, and possibly tome four or five will be up too.

The basic flow is to start in axe/torch, use Mantra of Flame, use skill two and skill four, then swap to tome and cast two, four, and five. There is some minor order preference where four and five are slightly preferred first, but doing two first is fine. Then swap back and continue as usual. Every time Symbol of Vengeance is up, use it, use skill four, then go back into tome for skill two and skill four or five if they are up. Try to complete your autoattack chains and do not rush the skill two and skill four combo. Let your tome cooldown come back, then use tome.

The interesting thing about the five-button version is that it gives Ashes of the Just to yourself and your allies. If you are playing in a group, you apply Ashes of the Just whenever you use tome skill five, and you also apply Ashes of the Just if you grant quickness. Unless you are doing quickness stuff, you will not really proc it yourself, but the build will do more damage in a group than it does alone because your allies can proc Ashes of the Just on the enemy. The DPS is around 35k by yourself, but realistically it may be more like 36k or 37k in a group due to Ashes of the Just.

For the overall build breakdown, skill two is CC and skill three is CC. You will probably use skill two off cooldown for DPS, so try to save skill three for CC. Technically, using skill three is a tiny DPS increase, but since this is five-button land, I do not use it. Skill five is mostly a condition cleanse. The damage is not great, so use it if you need the cleanse and otherwise leave it alone.

Firebrand has a lot of utility in tomes. In Tome of Justice, skills two, four, and five are big damage, and skill three is also CC, so you can pop into tome and use skill three if you need a little more CC. In Tome of Resolve, skill two is a condition cleanse, skill four is pulsing healing, and skill five is a condition cleanse plus a unique healing effectiveness buff that can help your healer. It cleanses conditions and converts them to boons. If you are playing the tome build, you should have a few extra pages for utility, though not a lot.

Tome of Courage also has good utility. Skill three is a reflect, skill five gives stability and aegis, and skill four gives resistance. Be careful with skill five because it increases toughness, so there are situations where you may cause a boss to attack you or one of your allies if you give them toughness.

Trait-wise, there is not much to add. Using all your mantra charges, such as Mantra of Flame, gives you more pages. Quickfire is relevant if you apply quickness, because granting quickness also grants Ashes of the Just. Apart from that, the traits are mostly just damage.

For utilities, you probably never want to swap Litany of Wrath. Mantra of Solace is not that useful here, though you can run it if you want more pages. Mantra of Flame should never be swapped. I left the elite on Signet of Courage, but you can take other skills. "Feel My Wrath!" gives superspeed and a little quickness, and you can use it passively to trigger Quickfire on the DPS build. Mantra of Liberation is a stability skill and is useful if your group needs stability.

If you swap a utility skill, I recommend swapping Bane Signet, but remember that Bane Signet is also CC. Firebrand has a lot of useful utilities. Judge's Intervention blinks to an opponent. Merciful Intervention blinks to an ally. Wall of Reflection is a reflect. You already have Tome of Courage skill three for reflect, but Wall of Reflection is more consistent if you need the reflect at a specific time and do not want to accidentally run out of pages. Purging Flames is the best sixth skill if you want to increase DPS on the five-button build. It was nerfed, but I still think it is best in slot for the condi build. Hallowed Ground and "Stand Your Ground!" are good stability skills, though I would consider Mantra of Liberation if you really need stability. Sanctuary is a strong CC skill because every pulse does breakbar damage.

If you want to min-max and squeeze out as much damage as possible, the best sixth button is Purging Flames. You can also take "Feel My Wrath!" to apply quickness.

To play this build as quickness, you need to replace at least two skills. Replace Bane Signet with Mantra of Potence, which is the quickness mantra, and replace your elite with "Feel My Wrath!", which is a quickness elite. Spam both of those skills off cooldown.

Firebrand cannot maintain 100% quickness uptime with zero boon duration as it is now, so you need to change your gear. One option is full Ritualist's gear, which gives vitality, condition damage, concentration, and expertise. Concentration gives boon duration. A second option is Superior Rune of the Firebrand instead of Superior Rune of the Trapper. You lose some condition duration and condition damage, but gain quickness duration. It is easy to acquire, lets you keep most of your gear as Viper's, and gives a 40% boon duration baseline. A third option is Superior Sigil of Concentration. You should probably replace the 5% damage sigil rather than the burning duration sigil, because burning duration is strong for this build.

At bare minimum, you want 40% boon duration. I recommend aiming for 50% to 60%, especially if you are playing it as low intensity, so you comfortably maintain quickness. The normal quickness build often swaps Unrelenting Criticism for Liberator's Vow, which grants quickness when you use a heal skill, and then uses Mantra of Solace with proper timing. But since this build uses only axe/torch, losing Unrelenting Criticism is a big hit. I would not recommend taking Liberator's Vow here. I would stick to Unrelenting Criticism, add boon duration, and run something like Litany of Wrath.

To recap: option one is Ritualist's gear, option two is Superior Rune of the Firebrand instead of Superior Rune of the Trapper, and option three is Superior Sigil of Concentration. Try to reach 50% to 60% boon duration to be comfortable. Then you can maintain quickness by using Mantra of Potence and "Feel My Wrath!" while playing the rest of the build normally.

items.title: 4 Button Firebrand Quickness Healer
items.cleaned_transcript:
For the build rundown, the build is pretty simple. All you really want to do is spam your heal, Mantra of Potence, "Advance!", and "Feel My Wrath!" off cooldown, then autoattack with hammer.

It is hard to make low-intensity healer builds because they will not work everywhere. I focus on giving as much passive healing as possible so you do not need to press your buttons for the most part. There will be fights where you need burst healing, so if you can, please run staff as your offhand weapon set if you decide to run this build. Staff is very good. Staff three, staff four, and staff two are all strong skills. Unfortunately, staff does not have as much passive healing as hammer, so I seriously recommend running staff as the second weapon set.

The way the build works is that the Virtues line improves your virtue skill two passive healing and grants it to nearby allies. You and your allies end up healing passively a lot more. Honor is mostly focused on improving healing, giving a little might, and Writ of Persistence. This is how the build heals for the most part: you heal through your symbols on the ground from hammer. A good majority of your healing comes from that.

You also grant might whenever you use up all of your mantra charges because Weighty Terms applies slow, which in turn triggers Stoic Demeanor. This build runs a few interesting skills. "Advance!" is usually covered through staff three, but hammer has more passive healing than staff, so your only source of swiftness is "Advance!". Your elite is a small source of quickness, but the main thing your elite is used for is fury. This build does not have fury generation outside of your elite and Relic of Altruism. Relic of Altruism grants fury and eight stacks of might whenever you use a healing skill. Your elite and Relic of Altruism are how you keep permanent fury uptime, which is important.

I would consider running staff, and I would also consider looking through your tome skills and using them. In these Firebrand builds, I have not used tomes much because I viewed tomes as weapon-swap-ish when I started making them, and these builds do not weapon swap. But all of your tomes are free to use. Useful tome skills include Tome of Justice skill three, which is a pull. Tome of Resolve is your main healing tome. If you are taking a lot of damage and do not want to run staff, Tome of Resolve skill five and skill four are big healing skills, and skill two is a condition cleanse. Tome of Courage can grant stability, protection, aegis, reflect, and pulsing resistance. I highly encourage you to either run staff offhand or use your tome skills. If you do neither, I think hammer is your best option.

For utility skills, you mostly only have one utility slot you can swap around. Hammer two is a little mobility. Hammer three technically procs Stoic Demeanor, so if you want a little resistance and might, you can use hammer three. Hammer four is CC.

I put Sanctuary on this build by default. Sanctuary is a very strong CC skill. If you put Sanctuary down on a boss with a defiance bar, you will see the breakbar go down. It does a lot of breakbar damage over a decent amount of time, and it is improved by Master of Consecrations. I would probably just run it.

If you want other strong options, Mantra of Lore is strong condition removal. Judge's Intervention blinks to an enemy. Merciful Intervention blinks to an ally and revives them. Wall of Reflection is self-explanatory. Hallowed Ground and "Stand Your Ground!" are strong stability skills. Sanctuary is the CC option I already covered. You can run Bane Signet if you need a little more CC, but you should probably prefer Sanctuary. If you need more healing and are not willing to use other options, Bow of Truth is solid because it is a large AoE heal.

With healer builds, you need to be somewhat dynamic based on what is happening in the fight. There will be parts where people take a lot of damage and you need to burst heal quickly to get them back up. For low-intensity healer builds, I am focusing more on strong passive healing, but keep in mind that some fights will require you to press the right burst-healing or utility buttons. I hope you enjoy the build.
